sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.61 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Suppose that$H$is a proper subgroup of a group$G$,$a H$,$a^2 1$and for every$g G H$the subgroup$< a,\, a^g >$is a Frobenius group whose complement contains$a$. Does the set-theoretic union of the kernels of all Frobenius subgroups of$G$with complement$< a >$constitute a subgroup? For definitions see 6.55; see also (A.\,I.\,Sozutov, Algebra and Logic, 34, no.\,5 (1995), 295--305). Editors' comment ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
